prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph. Same California suburban kitchen, evening 5:45 PM. Warm late-afternoon west-facing sunlight streams through the kitchen window, casting long golden diffused rays across the space. A Samsung Family Hub smart refrigerator (large touchscreen embedded in the door) glows with a blue-white screen light — the screen shows blurred question marks and a generic recipe list (out of focus). The refrigerator door is partially open, revealing it is packed full with food — yogurt containers, vegetables, proteins, condiments. The fridge is overflowing with ingredients, yet the screen looks lost and generic. The contrast between the screen's cool blue-white glow and the warm sunset light creates visual tension. Color grading: warm evening golden hour tones, but film-like desaturation keeps it from being too vivid. Shadows are warm but transparent. Composition: 16:9 wide frame. The Family Hub screen is the focal point (center-left). The kitchen wall and window with sunset light provide generous warm negative space on the right. Camera: 50mm prime lens equivalent, f/2.8, ISO 300. Fine film grain. Screen reflections scatter subtle light onto surrounding surfaces. Old-lens peripheral softness. The mood is 'capable but clueless' — a new beginning where technology is present but lacks family context. Hopeful uncertainty.
negative_prompt: dramatic backlighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated, CGI, plastic skin, studio lighting, dark room, horror, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, digital painting, anime, cartoon, illustration, sharp digital look
